ps4_mode_citation: GUIDE: ps4_mode: "proband_count"; ps4_mode_rationale: The SERPINC1 VCEP specification for PS4 explicitly requires the use of a proband point system to determine the evidence strength.
ps4_thresholds_applied:
- 1 point is awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL confirmed on repeated independent samples (Antithrombin deficiency PP4/PS4 Rule Specification Guidance, Proband Points).
- 1 point is awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL without repeat confirmation but with a family history of antithrombin activity levels < 0.8 IU/mL.
- 1 point is awarded for each proband with an abnormal crossed immunoelectrophoresis assay demonstrating decreased antithrombin function.
- 0.5 points are awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL without confirmation of repeated independent samples.
Strength levels are assigned based on total proband points:
  • PS4_Supporting: 1 point
  • PS4_Moderate: 2–3 points
  • PS4 (Strong): 4–7 points
  • PS4_VeryStrong: 8 or more points
